description_long: This is a traditional Chinese decorative hanging lantern, often used during festive occasions such as Lunar New Year. The lantern features an octagonal red plaque at the top, adorned with intricate gold patterns and Chinese characters, likely symbolizing good fortune or prosperity. Below the plaque, a series of cylindrical firecracker-shaped ornaments are suspended in a cascading arrangement, each decorated with red and gold motifs. The lantern is finished with a red tassel at the bottom, adding to its festive appearance. The entire structure is connected by a central red string or cord, with a loop at the top for hanging. The materials appear lightweight, primarily paper or thin cardboard with some fabric and possibly plastic for reinforcement.
description: A festive Chinese hanging lantern with an octagonal plaque, cascading firecracker-shaped ornaments, and a red tassel, richly decorated in red and gold.
description_view_0: Front view showing the octagonal plaque with gold patterns and Chinese characters, and the full arrangement of firecracker-shaped ornaments and tassel.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow profile of the lantern, with the firecracker shapes aligned in a vertical row and the octagonal plaque barely visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but with less detail visible on the plaque, still showing the cascading firecracker ornaments and tassel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the vertical alignment of the firecracker shapes.
